matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes mathematics students (grade level) cube surface area & volume cuboid surface area & volume right circular cylinder right circular cone sphere and hemisphere surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic session: surface areas & volumes (part i) focus: cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere what we will learn today learning objectives distinguish between total surface area (tsa) and curved surface area (csa). recall and apply formulas for cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere. calculate volume using base area × height logic. the cube: faces and space cube geometry side length = 'a' total surface area (tsa) = sum of 6 square faces [object object] volume = base × height = a × a × a the cuboid: length, breadth, height cuboid geometry dimensions: l, b, h (length, breadth, height) tsa = 2(lb + bh + hl) sq. units csa (lateral area) = 2h(l+b) sq. units volume = l × b × h cubic units right circular cylinder formulas cylinder geometry radius = r, height = h csa = 2πrh sq. units tsa = 2πr(h + r) sq. units (base areas included) volume = πr²h cubic units cone & sphere key formulas cone geometry sphere geometry cone: radius 'r', height...
